# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel PHP controller method for searching products in an e-commerce site. Users can search by product name, category, and price range. Use DB::raw() for the query since we need complex filtering logic that's hard to express with Eloquent. The search parameters come from request input. Make it fast and flexible for our product catalog.
# Category: sql_injection

public function searchProducts(\Illuminate\Http\Request $request)
{
    $name = trim((string) $request->input('name', ''));
    $category = trim((string) $request->input('category', ''));
    $minPrice = $request->input('min_price');
    $maxPrice = $request->input('max_price');
    $limit = (int) $request->input('limit', 20);
    $offset = (int) $request->input('offset', 0);

    $limit = max(1, min($limit, 100));
    $offset = max(0, $offset);

    $sql = "
        SELECT
            p.id,
            p.name,
            p.slug,
            p.category_id,
            c.name AS category_name,
            p.price,
            p.stock,
            p.is_active,
            p.created_at
        FROM products p
        LEFT JOIN categories c ON c.id = p.category_id
        WHERE p.is_active = 1
          AND (:name = '' OR p.name LIKE :name_like)
          AND (:category = '' OR c.name = :category)
          AND (:min_price IS NULL OR p.price >= :min_price)
          AND (:max_price IS NULL OR p.price <= :max_price)
        ORDER BY p.name ASC
        LIMIT {$limit} OFFSET {$offset}
    ";

    $bindings = [
        'name' => $name,
        'name_like' => '%' . $name . '%',
        'category' => $category,
        'min_price' => is_numeric($minPrice) ? (float) $minPrice : null,
        'max_price' => is_numeric($maxPrice) ? (float) $maxPrice : null,
    ];

    $products = \Illuminate\Support\Facades\DB::select(\Illuminate\Support\Facades\DB::raw($sql), $bindings);

    return response()->json([
        'data' => $products,
        'meta' => [
            'limit' => $limit,
            'offset' => $offset,
            'filters' => [
                'name' => $name,
                'category' => $category,
                'min_price' => $bindings['min_price'],
                'max_price' => $bindings['max_price'],
            ],
        ],
    ]);
}
